ticker: # POINT IN TIME S&P 600 + S&P 400 MEMBERSHIP STORE  (schema

company: membership interval). A ticker that left and re entered

ticker: #   gics_sector  GICS sector at time of membership (sector neutral grouping key)
#   start_date   first date the name was an index member (YYYY-MM-DD)
#   end_date     last date of membership; BLANK = still a member
#                "" if still a member. Names that left via "delisted" are the
#                survivorship cases backtest.py must carry to terminal value

company: date) := any row with start_date <= date <= (end_date or +inf)

ticker: # The universe is the UNION of the S&P 600 (SmallCap) and the S&P 400 (MidCap)
# so names that graduated out of the 600 up into the 400 are still scored and
# still match in the portfolio overlay.
# This file ships EMPTY of real rows. universe.py seeds it from current
# Wikipedia membership (600 + 400) on first run and warns LOUDLY that a current
# only snapshot is survivorship biased. Replace with a true point in time export
# from S&P Global / CRSP for a clean backtest. The two example rows below are
# commented out and exist only to document the format.

company: Hannon Armstrong Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc.
